hypothesis: A coordinated multi-agent protocol in ALP configuration III consisting of eight LLM agents initialized with systematically varied model temperatures and distinct prompt priors and overseen by a summarizing meta-agent that assigns unexplored regions will, using the same cumulative experimental time as a single-agent baseline, produce at least 30% greater coverage of the UMAP-projected experimental state space and reliably identify all four target reaction modes (ALD, ALE, ASD passivation, and CVD decomposition/co-dose) within fewer experimental iterations than the single-agent approach.
